name: astrapy (DataStax Astra DB Python Client)
pricing_notes: Astra DB: free tier (5GB, 20M ops/month); Pro from $0.25/GB; DataStax managed Cassandra+Vector

performance.notes: REST/HTTP-based Cassandra; higher latency than direct Cassandra driver but simpler ops

agent_readiness.notes: DataStax Astra DB (managed Cassandra + vector DB) Python client. from astrapy import DataAPIClient. client = DataAPIClient('AstraCS:token...'). db = client.get_database_by_api_endpoint('https://id-region.apps.astra.datastax.com'). coll = db.get_collection('my_collection'). coll.insert_one({'name': 'Alice', '$vector': [0.1, 0.2, ...]}). coll.find_one({'name': 'Alice'}). Vector search: coll.find(sort={'$vector': query_vector}, limit=5). coll.update_one({'_id': id}, {'$set': {'name': 'Bob'}}). coll.delete_one({'_id': id}). Async: AsyncDataAPIClient. Astra DB uses Data API (JSON/REST-based). Create collections with and without vector dimensions.

gotchas: Astra DB API is JSON/REST-based, not native Cassandra protocol — higher latency
Vector dimensions must be set at collection creation and cannot be changed
Free tier has limited ops/month; production apps need paid plan
Collection-based (document store) not table-based like raw Cassandra
astrapy v1 is a major rewrite from v0 — check migration guide
best_when: Managed vector+document database without operating Cassandra
RAG applications needing vector search + document storage
Serverless Cassandra-backed apps on Astra DB
When LangChain/LlamaIndex Astra integration is used
avoid_when: Low-latency requirements (REST overhead vs native protocol)
summary: astrapy is the DataStax Astra DB Python client for managed Cassandra + vector search. from astrapy import DataAPIClient; db = client.get_database_by_api_endpoint(url); coll = db.get_collection('col'); coll.insert_one({'$vector': [0.1,...], 'text': '...'}); coll.find(sort={'$vector': query}, limit=5). Async. REST-based. Freemium. v1 is a major rewrite. Best for RAG with managed Cassandra. Use cassandra-driver for native protocol.
